name: Chris Rose
focus: Instrument engineering • Remote sensing hardware • Planetary mission support
bio: Chris Rose is an instrument engineer at Northern Arizona University, where they work within a planetary science research group focused on developing advanced remote sensing instruments for drone and space exploration. Their long spaceflight history contributes to the design, testing, and implementation of instrumentation used to study planetary surfaces, through creating various thermal infrared instruments and supporting mechanisms. Their work supports NASA and international missions by helping translate scientific goals, project and contract requirements into functional hardware and data collection systems. They also support the various principal investigators with developing fast turn concept models, proposal scoping, and program management.
